headline: Jean-Luc Mélenchon announces fourth presidential run
description: Despite the emergence of several figures within La France Insoumise and his promise to step aside after the 2022 election, in which he gathered 21.95% of the vote, the populist leader announced his candidacy on Sunday.

headline: Trump faces a painful political reality, from Iran stalemate to legal setbacks
Hantavirus poses low risk to the public, WHO Europe says. What is the disease and where does it come from?
Saint Pierre and Miquelon, a pioneering example of climate change adaptation
description: This year, residents of a town in the French overseas territory off the coast of Canada are set to move into a new village. This relocation, France's first planned effort to move an entire town, is the result of a lengthy process.

headline: Jacques Garcia, designer of French opulence
description: He rose to fame in the 1990s by designing the interior of Hôtel Costes in Paris. Criticized by purists, his taste for antiques and lavish style won him success with wealthy private clients and luxury hotels.

headline: Paul Seixas, French cycling prodigy, to participate in first Tour de France
description: The 19-year-old cyclist is set to take part in the three-week race this summer. Since the start of the season, his performances have made him a serious contender for the podium.

headline: French Interior Minister Nuñez: 'I have no problem with Islam in France. But I fight those who use it to undermine our Republic'
description: Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez shares his view on the relationship between Islam and the French Republic. He reveals the contents of a bill he is presenting, aimed at combating 'separatism' and 'entryism.'

Laure Ferrari, the Frenchwoman whispering in Nigel Farage's ear
description: Little-known in France, Ferrari works as an adviser to her partner Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, currently leading the polls in the UK.

headline: Wales's pro-independence party hopes to top polls for first time ever
description: On May 7, regional and local elections in Wales could see the Labour Party suffer a historic defeat, leaving the pro-independence Plaid Cymru and the far-right Reform UK in a tight race for control of the Welsh government.

headline: Craving kouign-amann? Five standout bakeries in Brittany
description: From Douarnenez to Rennes, Le Monde toured Brittany's bakeries and pastry shops offering the most tempting takes on this beloved cake enjoyed around the globe.
